definition: A physician; a member of the medical profession; one who is trained and licensed to heal the sick or injured. The final examination and qualification may award a doctor degree in which case the post-nominal letters are D.O., DPM, M.D., DMD, DDS, in the US or MBBS in the UK.
A person who teaches, especially one employed in a school.
A person who creates art.
The presiding cook in the kitchen of a large household.
A person who steers a ship, a helmsman.
A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.
A wet nurse.
A person who, or thing that, protects or watches over something.
A male member of a monastic order who has devoted his life for religious service.
A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy. If it's an absolute monarchy, then he is the supreme ruler of his nation.
A female monarch. Example: Queen Victoria.
A person sent out to gain and bring in tidings; especially, one employed in war to gain information about the enemy and ground.
A person who performs, plays a part in a theatrical play or film.
A wheeled vehicle, generally drawn by horse power.
Someone who dives, especially as a sport.
A participant (fighter) in a boxing match.
One who shoots an arrow from a bow or a bolt from a crossbow.
One who hunts game for sport or for food; a huntsman or huntswoman.
A person who works the land and/or who keeps livestock, especially on a farm.
A young servant or follower; a trained military attendant in service of a lord.
A manservant having charge of wines and liquors.
A person in the business of navigating ships or other vessels
One who occupationally works with records, accounts, letters, etc.; an office worker.
One who ranges; a rover.
Someone, usually male, who uses (or has skill with) magic, mystic items, and magical and mystical practices.
